label: Category four Tropical Cyclone Narelle strikes Queensland causing widespread infrastructure damage
label_zh: 四級熱帶氣旋「納雷爾」襲擊昆士蘭 造成廣泛基礎設施損毀

timeline.title: Category Four Tropical Cyclone Narelle Makes Landfall in Queensland Causing Significant Infrastructure Damage
timeline.title_zh: 四級熱帶氣旋「娜瑞爾」登陸昆士蘭，造成嚴重基礎設施破壞
timeline.sub_title: The severe system struck the Cape York Peninsula on Friday morning, bringing destructive winds and heavy rainfall that have triggered widespread power outages and forced emergency evacuations across remote northern communities.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 該嚴重氣旋於週五上午襲擊了約克角半島，帶來破壞性強風和強降雨，引發了廣泛的停電，並迫使偏遠北部社區進行緊急疏散。
timeline.summary:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle officially made landfall on the Cape York Peninsula on Friday morning, striking the Queensland coast as a top-end category four system. The Bureau of Meteorology confirmed that the eye of the storm crossed the coast between Lockhart River and Cape Melville at approximately 7:00 AM local time. [ref:7] Although the system had intensified to a category five status while over the Coral Sea, it underwent a slight weakening phase just prior to reaching land. [ref:6, 3] Despite this, the cyclone remains a catastrophic event, packing sustained winds of 195 km/h—only 5 km/h shy of the category five threshold—and destructive gusts reaching as high as 270 km/h.

The immediate impact of the storm has been felt most acutely in remote northern communities. The town of Coen, which was situated directly in the cyclone’s path, has reported significant structural damage and widespread utility failures. Queensland [ref:6] Premier David Crisafulli confirmed that hundreds of power outages were recorded shortly after landfall, with dozens of homes in Coen losing electricity immediately. Infrastructure damage is expected to be extensive, with reports of uprooted trees, stripped vegetation, and damage to older properties that lack modern cyclone-rated protections. Essential services, including water and communications, have been disrupted in several areas, and 14 schools have been closed as a precautionary measure.

Environmental conditions accompanying the landfall have significantly increased the risk to life and property. Intense rainfall between 100 and 350 mm is expected across the peninsula, threatening to trigger severe flash flooding in catchments that were already saturated by earlier seasonal rains. Coastal regions are also facing the dual threat of a dangerous storm surge and abnormally high tides. In Cairns [ref:7], waves exceeding four meters were recorded, representing some of the highest surges in the region's history. Authorities have warned that the timing of the landfall with the high tide has exacerbated the risk of coastal inundation for communities south of the eye.

Logistical challenges are currently complicating the emergency response. The Cape York Peninsula is known for its sparse weather monitoring infrastructure, which has forced meteorologists to rely heavily on satellite imagery to track the storm's exact movements. In anticipation of the disaster, the federal government established a national fuel supply taskforce to ensure that regional areas remain supplied with petrol and diesel for emergency generators. This is particularly critical as many communities face the prospect of being isolated by floodwaters for days or even weeks. 

As of Friday afternoon, Cyclone Narelle continues its rapid westward trajectory across the peninsula. While the system is slowly losing its core intensity over land, it is forecast to maintain destructive strength as it traverses the region. The Bureau of [ref:6, 3, 10, 7] Meteorology predicts that Narelle will emerge into the Gulf of Carpentaria by Friday night, where warm waters are likely to cause the system to reintensify before it reaches the Northern Territory coast. Residents in the path of the storm are urged to remain in secure shelter until an official all-clear is issued by emergency services.
timeline.summary_zh:
嚴重熱帶氣旋「娜瑞爾」週五上午正式在約克角半島登陸，以最高級別的四級系統襲擊昆士蘭海岸。氣象局證實，風暴眼於當地時間上午 7 點左右在洛克哈特河 (Lockhart River) 和梅爾維爾角 (Cape Melville) 之間 crossing the coast。儘管該系統在珊瑚海上方增強為五級，但在登陸前稍有減弱。儘管如此，該氣旋仍然是一個災難性事件，持續風速達到每小時 195 公里——僅比五級標準低 5 公里/小時——並伴有高達每小時 270 公里的破壞性陣風。

風暴的直接影響在偏遠北部社區感受最為強烈。科恩 (Coen) 鎮位於氣旋路徑的正前方，報告了重大的結構損壞和廣泛的公用設施故障。昆士蘭州長大衛·克里薩菲利 (David Crisafulli) 證實，登陸後不久記錄到數百次停電，科恩鎮數十戶家庭立即失去電力。預計基礎設施損壞將非常廣泛，有報導稱樹木被連根拔起，植被被剝離，以及缺乏現代風暴等級保護的老舊建築物遭到損壞。幾個地區的基本服務，包括水電和通訊，都已中斷，14 所學校已作為預防措施關閉。

登陸伴隨的環境條件顯著增加了生命和財產的風險。預計該半島將出現 100 至 350 毫米的強降雨，可能引發已因早期季節性降雨而飽和的集水區嚴重閃洪。沿海地區也面臨危險的風暴潮和異常高潮的雙重威脅。在凱恩斯 (Cairns)，記錄到的海浪超過四米，代表該地區歷史上一些最高的海潮。

物流挑戰目前正使應急響應複雜化。約克角半島以其稀疏的天氣監測基礎設施而聞名，這迫使氣象學家主要依靠衛星圖像來追蹤風暴的確切移動。為應對這次災難，聯邦政府成立了一個國家燃料供應特別工作組，以確保地區能夠獲得汽油和柴油以供應急發電機使用。這尤其關鍵，因為許多社區可能在數天甚至數週內被洪水隔離。

截至週五下午，氣旋「娜瑞爾」繼續快速向西穿越半島。儘管風暴在陸地上緩慢失去核心強度，但預計在穿越該地區時仍將保持破壞性強度。氣象局預測「娜瑞爾」將於週五晚間進入卡奔塔利亞灣 (Gulf of Carpentaria)，那裡的溫暖水域可能會導致風暴重新增強，然後抵達北領地海岸。敦促風暴路徑上的居民留在安全的庇護所，直到緊急服務部門發布官方的解除警報。
